mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and recession probability, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of co-movement instances during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an economic expansion regime reflects strengthening output capacity and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically insignificant (Rank IC < 0.01) when tested exclusively within the early-cycle expansion regime over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 220.0 indicates entrenched inflation that forces central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 70 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0, or the threshold signal shows no incremental predictive power over a linear baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumption capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with payroll increases.

mechanism_narrative: High real GDP growth combined with low inflation creates a Goldilocks environment that boosts equity risk premiums and corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance or the joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 65 percent of Goldilocks instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening regime signals demand destruction and earnings recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns or shows positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations when tested exclusively within the monetary tightening regime.
